messages.text:
Hello All,

After fully curing the tensile bars we needed to tweak the formulation slightly by adding in 3wt% EGDMA. An interesting aspect we found was the amount of heat generation during the polymerization seems to heavily dictate the properties i.e. the amount of heat dissipation due to the amount of surface area is important. This is a common issue with polymers used in applications similar to this. If you need a more "sciency" explanation <@U0127BGJ3U5> can describe exactly the common issues as hes dug more into the literature than i have.

<@U012M8NDV3K> please include anything else i have missed

Bottom line, the only hesitation with sampling now is the risk that heat generation anticipated in a larger mass may cause embrittlement.

If there's time to test this we should. New Galata sample hasn't arrived yet

So here's kind of what I see as the summary and the options. Summary: We've produced something that meets the specs, but there are some potential variables based on our own discoveries. Namely, how much heat generation affects the properties. Options:
1. Spend some more time figuring out how much of an effect that has and see if it effects our formulation at all 
2. Let Polyset sample our MVP and get some feedback from them
I can see arguments either way. If you asked for my recommendation, I'd say let's do both. Talk with Polyset, give them a sample if they say they are ready to take it, and in the mean time, see if we can optimize a bit more.

<@U06F97BPQJU> Thoughts?
How long would 1. Take? What are the economics? If you need more time, we’ll just tell them, hopefully with an ETA on a sample.

• We're just waiting on some molds for option 1. They should be here next week, and it's pretty quick to turn that around. 
• I think that there's a little bit more that we can do with regards to formulating, but I would argue that we're quickly reaching the point where we need some feedback from the prospective customer/applicator
• The ballpark economics are <https://docs.google.com/document/d/1NCHAynS-GiivbVPR1kF-ujCAldz5QZ3cmfPU10OpGZQ/edit?pli=1|here>. I'm still waiting for actual numbers to come in. <@U012212P2PQ> has asked for pricing, and I believe he's still waiting on response from the vendors.
